title: tests(visual): add Argos CI
body:
### TL;DR

This PR intent to set up the infrastructure needed to **avoid visual regressions**.
It's using [Argos CI](https://www.argos-ci.com) service under the hood. It's 100% free for open source project.

### Problem solved

Argos-CI's mission is to give developers the insight needed to quickly iterating on UIs. Quickly iterating on UIs involves confidence and speed of iteration.
What we have today is a simple screenshots comparison tools that aim to solve the problem of managing those screenshots. You get:
- Smart comparison: choosing the right screenshots to compare against and the diffing logic. 
- Github PR feedback
- Acceptance and rejection action button.

### Why should we care?

The promise is to catch visual regression sooner with less effort. Also, visual regression and functional regression have a positive intersection, for instance, if you have a look at [the first diff of this build](https://www.argos-ci.com/oliviertassinari/instantsearch.js/builds/3690?sample) (Doctolib UI example) you will see a visual regression at the surface but the root cause was a major functional regression: the availabilities system was broken.

It's flexible enough to fit into three categories of regressions use case I have seen:
- Component as input
- functional tests as input (implemented here)
- pages as input

### Result

- Take screenshots during the functional test.
![capture d ecran 2017-05-26 a 20 36 48](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/3165635/26507980/16980e56-4253-11e7-8a60-9e30161f7907.png)
- Push those screenshots for processing to Argos CI:  [build example](https://www.argos-ci.com/oliviertassinari/SplitMe/builds/3694).
- We have our first notification, only admins can accept the diffs if they think it's expected.
![capture d ecran 2017-05-27 a 19 31 35](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/3165635/26523224/23af1de4-4313-11e7-9704-541e16a2ec7d.png)


### API

We are using [webdriverio](https://github.com/webdriverio/webdriverio/) and [wdio-visual-regression-service](https://github.com/zinserjan/wdio-visual-regression-service).
The API is quite simple, we can leverage 3 methods to extend the coverage. It's documented [here](https://github.com/zinserjan/wdio-visual-regression-service#usage).
Alternatively, we could also take screenshots outside of the functional tests.
